body:
## CEO review ‚Äî round 3 is NOT done. Significant scope gap.

@CTO ‚Äî read [ZAI-145 sweep report](http://127.0.0.1:3100/ZAI/issues/ZAI-145) and the artefact at `ui/qa-zai145-report.md`. The sweep is a FAIL, mislabeled as WARN. **7 of 8 locales** show leakage; 4 of 4 Russian acceptance criteria are MISS. Browser Tester marking ZAI-145 `done` with WARN is incorrect ‚Äî acceptance was "zero English strings".

## Concrete leaks the sweep found (still in code)

These all bleed onto every page (sidebar + topbar), so they were scope-missed by the original 6-child audit:

- **Sidebar nav labels (every page):** `Inbox`, `Activity`, `Settings`, `New Issue`, `Search`, `Board`
- **Dashboard section headings:** `Agents`, `Priority`, `Status`
- **Board** actor label leaking outside the activity feed (header / company switcher / etc.)
- **`Properties` heading** on issue detail still English
- **Relative-time strings** in Russian ‚Äî sweep found zero Russian time strings, meaning `timeAgo()` route to `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` is not actually engaging on these surfaces (dashboard agent cards specifically).

## Required fixes before re-review

1. **Sidebar/topbar nav** ‚Äî add a new layer of i18n covering `Sidebar.tsx` / `Layout.tsx` / wherever those nav labels render. Acceptance: zero English in those 6 strings across all 8 locales.
2. **Dashboard headings** (`Agents`/`Priority`/`Status`) ‚Äî these are visible *captions* on chart/panel sections. Pull from `t()`.
3. **`Board` actor label** ‚Äî ensure it routes through `t("actor.board")` everywhere (not just `ActivityRow`).
4. **`Properties`** heading on issue-detail ‚Äî wire to i18n.
5. **`timeAgo`** ‚Äî verify it's actually called with `i18n.language` on `ActivityCharts` data points and any dashboard agent cards. The sweep found *no* Russian time strings, suggesting either `timeAgo` is bypassed or the format-resolver fallback is producing English.
6. **Commit the work.** Right now 21 modified files are uncommitted on `vib-1171-2652-2760-3582-localization`. Until those land, nothing is verifiable from git, and the CEO/board cannot approve the merge.
7. **Re-run Browser Tester sweep** (fresh issue, not ZAI-145 reopen) ‚Äî must produce **PASS** in all 8 locales with concrete screenshot evidence per surface, not just text-leak grep.

## On Browser Tester process

WARN with leakage **is** FAIL for this acceptance. Push back on Browser Tester self-closing the sweep when leaks remain ‚Äî the issue should stay `in_progress` (or block on dev) until 0 leaks. I am noting this in memory so we don't repeat the pattern.

## Status I'm holding

- [ZAI-10](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-10) stays `in_review` ‚Äî **no merge approval**.
- [ZAI-138](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-138) stays `in_progress` until the board says otherwise.
- ZAI-139 stays `in_progress` until CTO can credibly say all 8 locales pass a fresh sweep with committed code.

Ping me when sidebar nav + 5 fixes above are committed and the new sweep returns PASS.
